title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout with the Providence Bruins and will debut against the Springfield Thunderbirds.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney indicated there is no rush for Hagens to join Boston, despite his potential as a top-six player.
Hagens' performance in Providence will determine if he moves to the NHL; Boston focuses on playoffs and team readiness.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, was sentenced to life without parole for the 1986 murder of Tracy Gilpin in Plymouth, MA.
Tracy Gilpin, 15, was found dead in Myles Standish State Park after going missing for 20 days in 1986.
The kidnapping and assault charges against Hand were dropped due to expired statute of limitations, but murder charges remained.

title: Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was struck and killed while crossing I-90 East in Ludlow, Massachusetts on Monday afternoon.
State Police closed all travel lanes for 30 minutes following the incident near the 54 mile marker.
The victim's identity had not been released by police as of late Monday afternoon.

title: Jayson Tatum’s son, Deuce, wrote a letter in Slam Magazine about Celtics star’s comeback
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum's son, Deuce, wrote a letter in Slam Magazine about his father's recovery from an Achilles' injury.
Deuce shared his emotions, from sadness during Tatum's injury to pride in his father's rehab efforts.
Tatum missed nearly ten months of the NBA season, returning to play against the Mavericks earlier this month.

title: 5 teens arrested after Panera brawl; BPD has responded to location more than 15 times this year, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Five teenagers arrested at Dorchester Panera for allegedly assaulting police and trespassing, BPD reported.
Incident marks at least 16 police responses to Dorchester Panera since January 1, 2026.
Teens charged include two 15-year-old girls, one 17-year-old girl, one 14-year-old boy, and another 15-year-old girl.

title: ‘I am scared for my safety’: Plymouth police officer placed on leave is accused of sexual abuse, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine, 31, is on paid leave after accusations of sexual abuse surfaced in court records.
Pelrine and her husband, Daniel Forand, are accused of sexually assaulting their legal ward over several years.
Both are prohibited from contacting the alleged victim and will appear in Plymouth District Court on March 27.

title: Testimony of officer who shot Kelsey Fitzsimmons continues on day 2 of trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, is on trial for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Officer Patrick Noonan testified about the June 2025 shooting, claiming he acted in self-defense when Fitzsimmons drew a gun.
The Essex County Superior Court trial includes witness testimony and evidence examination; proceedings will continue Wednesday.

title: After years of illness, Natick woman runs Boston Marathon with BAA
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Mackenzie Roy, 32, from Natick, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after battling autoimmune diseases for years.
Roy participates with B.A.A. Gives Back, an organization promoting community, health, and fitness.
Growing up watching the Marathon in Natick, Roy dreamed for 15 years of running the iconic 26.2-mile race.

title: Bruins’ top prospect James Hagens welcomes challenge as he begins pro career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, Boston Bruins prospect, starts pro career with Providence Bruins in an AHL tryout.
Selected seventh in the 2025 NHL Draft, Hagens aims to secure a spot in Boston's lineup.
Providence coach Ryan Mougenel emphasizes the AHL's role in Hagens' development and adherence to team standards.

title: What Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White said about their Monday Night Raw appearance
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White attended WWE's Monday Night Raw at TD Garden for family time and learning.
Mazzulla, an MMA enthusiast, enjoys exploring diverse arenas like WWE to find lessons applicable to coaching basketball.
White joked about a potential WrestleMania rematch, preferring to maintain his 'babyface' persona over a 'heel' role.
